macro_architecture.reasoning: 2 active containers (backend, frontend) with 738 total components. Backend uses Layered pattern, Frontend uses Component-Based pattern.

layer_structure: Business Logic / Service (Services, Guards, Resolvers, Interceptors)
Infrastructure / Integration (Adapters, Endpoint services, HTTP interceptors)

container_analyses.summary: The backend container implements a layered Spring Boot architecture (Presentation, Service, Repository, Domain) to support a legal deed and document management bounded context. It exposes REST APIs for external clients, handles business logic for deeds, actions, metadata, cryptographic key management, archiving, scheduled jobs, and reporting, and integrates with external key‑management and archiving services as well as a relational database via JPA/Hibernate. While the overall design follows solid architectural principles, moderate coupling and cohesion issues arise from oversized controllers, mixed service/DAO responsibilities, and a sprawling domain model, leading to a quality grade of B.

capabilities: Create, retrieve and manage deed entries
Log and track deed actions and connections
Execute scheduled jobs and background processing
Expose RESTful APIs for external clients

debt_indicators: Controllers with many endpoints (potentially large controllers)
Service layer contains DAO implementations (mixing data‑access responsibilities)
High number of entity classes (possible duplication or overly fine‑grained model)
Missing explicit interfaces for external integrations (e.g., Key Management, Archiving)
Potential God‑service classes handling multiple unrelated concerns
recommendations: Refactor large controller classes into smaller, purpose‑focused controllers
Introduce service interfaces and move DAO implementations out of the service layer
Group related entities into sub‑domains or packages to improve domain cohesion
Create abstraction layers (facade or adapter) for external services like XnpKmService and Archiving
Apply DDD bounded‑context boundaries to limit the size of the domain model
Review and reduce the number of service classes by consolidating similar business logic

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The backend demonstrates a well‑structured layered approach and comprehensive functional coverage, but moderate technical debt in controller and service design hampers maintainability and scalability. Addressing the identified coupling and cohesion issues will elevate the quality from B to an A‑level rating.
top_recommendations: Refactor large controller classes into smaller, purpose‑focused controllers
Introduce service interfaces and separate DAO implementations from business services
Create abstraction (facade/adapter) layers for external Key Management and Archiving services

container_analyses.summary: The frontend container is built on a component‑based, layered Angular architecture that cleanly separates presentation, business‑logic, and infrastructure concerns using a rich set of Angular technologies (NgModules, RxJS, Angular Material, etc.). It supports a wide range of deed‑registry capabilities through REST APIs and local storage. While the overall design is solid, the codebase shows moderate coupling and cohesion, with duplication in services, adapters, and pipes, leading to a quality grade of C and a noticeable technical debt surface.

layers: Business Logic / Service (Services, Guards, Resolvers, Interceptors)
Infrastructure / Integration (Adapters, Endpoint services, HTTP interceptors)

capabilities: Create, edit, view, and delete deed entries
Display deed overviews and status headers
Import and export deed data (JSON, PDF)
Apply form validation and field validation
Handle error reporting, rate limiting and busy indicators
Persist user preferences in local storage

debt_indicators: Versioned ImportHandler services indicating code duplication
Many adapters acting as thin wrappers without clear abstraction
recommendations: Consolidate similar services and adapters into shared abstractions
Introduce facade or interface layers to reduce direct component‑service coupling
Group related pipes into dedicated utility modules or libraries
Refactor versioned ImportHandler services using a strategy or plugin pattern
Enforce stricter feature‑module boundaries to keep presentation, business, and infrastructure layers separate
Add explicit TypeScript interfaces for services to enable dependency inversion and easier testing

container_analyses.overall_assessment: The frontend container delivers the required domain functionality with a well‑defined layered Angular stack, but moderate coupling, duplicated services, and fragmented utilities inflate maintenance effort. The C grade reflects this debt; targeted refactoring and clearer module boundaries will raise both quality and developer productivity.
top_recommendations: Consolidate similar services and adapters into shared abstractions
Refactor versioned ImportHandler services using a strategy or plugin pattern
Enforce stricter feature‑module boundaries to maintain clear separation of concerns

layers: Business Logic / Service (Services, Guards, Resolvers, Interceptors)
Infrastructure / Integration (Adapters, Endpoint services, HTTP interceptors)

executive_summary: uvz is a modular monolith system with 738 components across 2 active containers: backend (333 components, Grade B), frontend (404 components, Grade C). Overall architecture grade: C.
